pattern: Extend arithmetic runs incrementally: each position that continues the previous common difference adds a number of new slices equal to the current run length.
hints: An arithmetic subarray of length L contains several shorter arithmetic subarrays ending at its last element; count them as you grow each run.
Look at consecutive differences: a position continues an arithmetic run only if its difference equals the previous one.
Keep a counter of how many arithmetic subarrays end at the current index; when the difference continues, increment it and add it to the total, otherwise reset it to zero.

edge_inputs.description: two elements cannot form a slice
the difference breaks partway, splitting into separate runs
constant sequence is arithmetic with difference zero

stress.description: random values with occasional planted runs
stress.note: mixes structured arithmetic runs with noise
